revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]建立内容为“{{Not|龙首寺}}[[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|霞浦县博物馆中展出的乐山堂遗址照片]] '''乐山堂'''，俗称'''盖竹堂'''，旧称'''龙首寺'''，位于中国[[福建省]][[宁德市]][[霞浦县]][[柏洋乡]][[塔后村]][[上万村|上万自然村]]，由福建[[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）教徒孙绵于[[北宋]][[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）建立，是福建境内最早兴建的摩…”的新页面
revision.text:
{{Not|龙首寺}}[[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|霞浦县博物馆中展出的乐山堂遗址照片]] '''乐山堂'''，俗称'''盖竹堂'''，旧称'''龙首寺'''，位于中国[[福建省]][[宁德市]][[霞浦县]][[柏洋乡]][[塔后村]][[上万村|上万自然村]]，由福建[[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）教徒孙绵于[[北宋]][[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）建立，是福建境内最早兴建的摩尼教建筑，一千多年来都是柏洋地区的摩尼教重要活动场所，直至2006年毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|桑美台风]]。<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref><ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>
== 名称 == 乐山堂最初建立时，被称作“龙首寺”，元代时改称“乐山堂”。最开始，乐山一词的命名由来被认为是取自《[[论语]]·壅也》当中的“智者乐水，仁者乐山”一句<ref>{{Cite book|title=欧亚学刊 第9辑：第二届传统中国研究国际学术讨论会欧亚专辑|last=Ma|first=Xiaohe|publisher=中华书局|year=2009|location=北京|pages=83-84|chapter=Remains of the Religion of Light in Xiapu County, Fujian Province|authorlink=马小鹤}}</ref>。后另有说法认为，乐山一词源自[[中古波斯语]]{{Lang|pal|Rōšan}}（或{{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}），意为“光明”<ref>{{Cite book|title=''A Concise Pahlavi Dictionary''|publisher=[[牛津大学出版社|Oxford University Press]]|year=1971|location=[[伦敦|London]]-[[纽约|New York]]-[[多伦多|Toronto]]|pages=72}}</ref>，与摩尼教崇尚光明的教义相呼应。此外，由于该建筑的性质更可能是教徒活动用的斋堂，而非修道用的寺院（见“建筑风格与性质”章节），故将“寺”更名为“堂”的原因很可能为此。<ref name=":0" />
== 历史沿革 == 据民国21年（1932年）纂修的《民国孙氏宗谱》的明确记载，禅洋村（今又称神洋村，隶属于柏洋乡）人[[孙绵]]在北宋乾德四年（966年）购置此地，并着手修建龙首寺，成为首任寺主。[[天圣]]五年（1027年），上万村人[[林瞪]]在龙首寺皈依明教，后成为当地的明教传教宗师、龙首寺第二代寺主，龙首寺在林瞪的主持下，影响力大大扩增。林瞪之后的寺主则是他的嫡传弟子陈平山。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>
元朝时期，龙首寺改名为乐山堂，明清时期，乐山堂曾两次重修。中华人民共和国成立初期，据当地老人回忆，乐山堂尚有几处院落。[[文化大革命]]初，乐山堂被拆毁，后简单地进行了修缮，搭起了棚式建筑，用于存放村民的农具和临时避雨。2006年，乐山堂毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|超强台风桑美]]，目前仅残存柱础、台阶、神龛底座等遗址。<ref name=":0" />
== 建筑风格与性质 == 由于年代久远、缺乏文字记录，乐山堂的原貌已无从得知。乐山堂遗址占地约1560平方米，从文革末期搭建的简易建筑照片来看，乐山堂依靠立柱支撑，四面并无墙壁。如果这种修筑方式与原建筑相同，那么乐山堂并不适合修行；且乐山堂修建在信徒易为集中村口，而非适合僧侣修行的偏僻地带，表明乐山堂并不具备寺院的一般特征，而更符合斋堂的特征。这也是龙首寺更名为乐山堂的可能原因之一。<ref name=":0" />
== 参考文献 == <references />{{摩尼教页脚}} [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:960年代建立]] [[Category:已不存在的建築物]] [[Category:霞浦县]]

{{Not|龙首寺}}[[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|霞浦县博物馆中展出的乐山堂遗址照片]] '''乐山堂'''，俗称'''盖竹堂'''，旧称'''龙首寺'''，位于中国[[福建省]][[宁德市]][[霞浦县]][[柏洋乡]][[塔后村]][[上万村|上万自然村]]，由福建[[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）教徒孙绵于[[北宋]][[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）建立，是福建境内最早兴建的摩尼教建筑，一千多年来都是柏洋地区的摩尼教重要活动场所，直至2006年毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|桑美台风]]。<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref><ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>

== 建筑风格与性质 == 由于年代久远、缺乏文字记录，乐山堂的原貌已无从得知。乐山堂遗址占地约1560平方米，从文革末期搭建的简易建筑照片来看，乐山堂依靠立柱支撑，四面并无墙壁。如果这种修筑方式与原建筑相同，那么乐山堂并不适合修行；且乐山堂修建在信徒易为集中的村口，而非适合僧侣修行的偏僻地带，表明乐山堂并不具备[[寺院]]的一般特征，而更符合斋堂的特征。这也是龙首寺更名为乐山堂的可能原因之一。<ref name=":0" />
== 参见 == * [[霞浦文书]]：记载霞浦摩尼教的重要文献集，是目前发现的篇幅最长的摩尼教汉文文献集，对乐山堂的记载见于文书的《乐山堂神记》。 * [[上万村摩尼教石雕造像]]：修建在乐山堂附近，用于装饰塔后三佛塔和瞪公石塔，是摩尼教存世不多的石雕艺术品。 * [[福寿宫]]：位于[[福州市]]南门外，是摩尼教保存最完好的宫庙建筑，宫内供奉的是曾在福州救灾的林瞪。
== 参考文献 == <references />{{摩尼教页脚}} [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:966年建立]] [[Category:福建已不存在的建筑物]] [[Category:霞浦县]] [[Category:中國已毀地標]]

revision.text:
{{Not|龙首寺}}[[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|霞浦县博物馆中展出的乐山堂遗址照片]] '''乐山堂'''，俗称'''盖竹堂'''，旧称'''龙首寺'''，位于中国[[福建省]][[宁德市]][[霞浦县]][[柏洋乡]][[塔后村]][[上万村|上万自然村]]，由福建[[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）教徒孙绵于[[北宋]][[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）建立，是福建境内最早兴建的摩尼教建筑，一千多年来都是柏洋地区的摩尼教重要活动场所，直至2006年毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|桑美台风]]。<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref><ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>
== 名称 == 乐山堂最初建立时，被称作“龙首寺”，元代时改称“乐山堂”。最开始，乐山一词的命名由来被认为是取自《[[论语]]·壅也》当中的“智者乐水，仁者乐山”一句<ref>{{Cite book|title=欧亚学刊 第9辑：第二届传统中国研究国际学术讨论会欧亚专辑|last=Ma|first=Xiaohe|publisher=中华书局|year=2009|location=北京|pages=83-84|chapter=Remains of the Religion of Light in Xiapu County, Fujian Province|authorlink=马小鹤}}</ref>。后另有说法认为，乐山一词源自[[中古波斯语]]{{Lang|pal|Rōšan}}（或{{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}），意为“光明”<ref>{{Cite book|title=''A Concise Pahlavi Dictionary''|publisher=[[牛津大学出版社|Oxford University Press]]|year=1971|location=[[伦敦|London]]-[[纽约|New York]]-[[多伦多|Toronto]]|pages=72}}</ref>，与摩尼教崇尚光明的教义相呼应。此外，由于该建筑的性质更可能是教徒活动用的斋堂，而非修道用的寺院（见“建筑风格与性质”章节），故将“寺”更名为“堂”的原因很可能为此。<ref name=":0" />
== 历史沿革 == 据民国21年（1932年）纂修的《民国孙氏宗谱》的明确记载，禅洋村（今又称神洋村，隶属于柏洋乡）人[[孙绵]]在北宋乾德四年（966年）购置此地，并着手修建龙首寺，成为首任寺主。[[天圣]]五年（1027年），上万村人[[林瞪]]在龙首寺皈依明教，后成为当地的明教传教宗师、龙首寺第二代寺主，龙首寺在林瞪的主持下，影响力大大扩增。林瞪之后的寺主则是他的嫡传弟子陈平山。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>
元朝时期，龙首寺改名为乐山堂，明清时期，乐山堂曾两次重修。中华人民共和国成立初期，据当地老人回忆，乐山堂尚有几处院落。[[文化大革命]]初，乐山堂被拆毁，后简单地进行了修缮，搭起了棚式建筑，用于存放村民的农具和临时避雨。2006年，乐山堂毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|超强台风桑美]]，目前仅残存柱础、台阶、神龛底座等遗址。<ref name=":0" />
== 建筑风格与性质 == 由于年代久远、缺乏文字记录，乐山堂的原貌已无从得知。乐山堂遗址占地约1560平方米，从文革末期搭建的简易建筑照片来看，乐山堂依靠立柱支撑，四面并无墙壁。如果这种修筑方式与原建筑相同，那么乐山堂并不适合修行；且乐山堂修建在信徒易为集中的村口，而非适合僧侣修行的偏僻地带，表明乐山堂并不具备[[寺院]]的一般特征，而更符合斋堂的特征。这也是龙首寺更名为乐山堂的可能原因之一。<ref name=":0" />
== 参见 == * [[霞浦文书]]：记载霞浦摩尼教的重要文献集，是目前发现的篇幅最长的摩尼教汉文文献集，对乐山堂的记载见于文书的《乐山堂神记》。 * [[上万村摩尼教石雕造像]]：修建在乐山堂附近，用于装饰塔后三佛塔和瞪公石塔，是摩尼教存世不多的石雕艺术品。 * [[福寿宫]]：位于[[福州市]]南门外，是摩尼教保存最完好的宫庙建筑，宫内供奉的是曾在福州救灾的林瞪。
== 参考文献 == <references />{{摩尼教页脚}} [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:966年建立]] [[Category:福建已不存在的建筑物]] [[Category:霞浦县历史]] [[Category:中國已毀地標]] [[Category:宁德宗教史]] [[Category:北宋建筑]] [[Category:北宋宗教]]

{{Not|龙首寺}}[[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|霞浦县博物馆中展出的乐山堂遗址照片]] '''乐山堂'''，俗称'''盖竹堂'''，旧称'''龙首寺'''，位于中国[[福建省]][[宁德市]][[霞浦县]][[柏洋乡]][[塔后村]][[上万村|上万自然村]]，由福建[[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）教徒孙绵于[[北宋]][[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）建立，是福建境内最早兴建的摩尼教建筑，一千多年来都是柏洋地区的摩尼教重要活动场所，直至2006年毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|桑美台风]]。<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref><ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>

== 历史沿革 == 据民国21年（1932年）纂修的《民国孙氏宗谱》的明确记载，禅洋村（今又称神洋村，隶属于柏洋乡）人[[孙绵]]在北宋乾德四年（966年）购置此地，并着手修建龙首寺，成为首任寺主。[[天圣]]五年（1027年），上万村人[[林瞪]]在龙首寺皈依明教，后成为当地的明教传教宗师、龙首寺第二代寺主，龙首寺在林瞪的主持下，影响力大大扩增。林瞪之后的寺主则是他的嫡传弟子陈平山。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>
元朝时期，龙首寺改名为乐山堂，明清时期，乐山堂曾两次重修。中华人民共和国成立初期，据当地老人回忆，乐山堂尚有几处院落。[[文化大革命]]初，乐山堂被拆毁，后简单地进行了修缮，搭起了棚式建筑，用于存放村民的农具和临时避雨。2006年，乐山堂毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|超强台风桑美]]，目前仅残存柱础、台阶、神龛底座等遗址。<ref name=":0" />
== 建筑风格与性质 == 由于年代久远、缺乏文字记录，乐山堂的原貌已无从得知。乐山堂遗址占地约1560平方米，从文革末期搭建的简易建筑照片来看，乐山堂依靠立柱支撑，四面并无墙壁。如果这种修筑方式与原建筑相同，那么乐山堂并不适合修行；且乐山堂修建在信徒易为集中的村口，而非适合僧侣修行的偏僻地带，表明乐山堂并不具备[[寺院]]的一般特征，而更符合斋堂的特征。这也是龙首寺更名为乐山堂的可能原因之一。<ref name=":0" />
== 相关活动 == 每年的中元节（农历七月十五），上万村的摩尼教信徒会集合在乐山堂举行超度亡人的仪式。这种超度亡灵的理念与崇尚光明的摩尼教教义相左，说明霞浦摩尼教为了在当地大众化、存续至今，已然融合进了一些类似这样超度亡灵的、能慰藉民众的独特内容。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>
== 参见 == * [[霞浦文书]]：记载霞浦摩尼教的重要文献集，是目前发现的篇幅最长的摩尼教汉文文献集，对乐山堂的记载见于文书的《乐山堂神记》。 * [[上万村摩尼教石雕造像]]：修建在乐山堂附近，用于装饰塔后三佛塔和瞪公石塔，是摩尼教存世不多的石雕艺术品。 * [[福寿宫]]：位于[[福州市]]南门外，是摩尼教保存最完好的宫庙建筑，宫内供奉的是曾在福州救灾的林瞪。
== 参考文献 == <references />{{摩尼教页脚}} [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:966年建立]] [[Category:福建已不存在的建筑物]] [[Category:霞浦县历史]] [[Category:中國已毀地標]] [[Category:宁德宗教史]] [[Category:北宋建筑]] [[Category:北宋宗教]] [[Category:宁德宗教建筑]]

{{Not|龙首寺}}[[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|霞浦县博物馆中展出的乐山堂遗址照片]] '''乐山堂'''，俗称'''盖竹堂'''，旧称'''龙首寺'''，位于中国[[福建省]][[宁德市]][[霞浦县]][[柏洋乡]][[塔后村]][[上万村|上万自然村]]，由福建[[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）教徒孙绵于[[北宋]][[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）建立，是福建境内最早兴建的摩尼教建筑，一千多年来都是柏洋地区的摩尼教重要活动场所，直至2006年毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|桑美台风]]。<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref><ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>
== 名称 == 乐山堂最初建立时，被称作“龙首寺”，元代时改称“乐山堂”。最开始，乐山一词的命名由来被认为是取自《[[论语]]·壅也》当中的“智者乐水，仁者乐山”一句<ref>{{Cite book|title=欧亚学刊 第9辑：第二届传统中国研究国际学术讨论会欧亚专辑|last=Ma|first=Xiaohe|publisher=中华书局|year=2009|location=北京|pages=83-84|chapter=Remains of the Religion of Light in Xiapu County, Fujian Province|authorlink=马小鹤|language=en}}</ref>。后另有说法认为，乐山一词源自[[中古波斯语]]{{Lang|pal|Rōšan}}（或{{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}），意为“光明”<ref>{{Cite book|title=''A Concise Pahlavi Dictionary''|publisher=[[牛津大学出版社|Oxford University Press]]|year=1971|location=[[伦敦|London]]-[[纽约|New York]]-[[多伦多|Toronto]]|pages=72|language=en}}</ref>，与摩尼教崇尚光明的教义相呼应。此外，由于该建筑的性质更可能是教徒活动用的斋堂，而非修道用的寺院（见“建筑风格与性质”章节），故将“寺”更名为“堂”的原因很可能为此。<ref name=":0" />
== 历史沿革 == 据民国21年（1932年）纂修的《民国孙氏宗谱》的明确记载，禅洋村（今又称神洋村，隶属于柏洋乡）人[[孙绵]]在北宋乾德四年（966年）购置此地，并着手修建龙首寺，成为首任寺主。[[天圣]]五年（1027年），上万村人[[林瞪]]在龙首寺皈依明教，后成为当地的明教传教宗师、龙首寺第二代寺主，龙首寺在林瞪的主持下，影响力大大扩增。林瞪之后的寺主则是他的嫡传弟子陈平山。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>
元朝时期，龙首寺改名为乐山堂，明清时期，乐山堂曾两次重修。中华人民共和国成立初期，据当地老人回忆，乐山堂尚有几处院落。[[文化大革命]]初，乐山堂被拆毁，后简单地进行了修缮，搭起了棚式建筑，用于存放村民的农具和临时避雨。2006年，乐山堂毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|超强台风桑美]]，目前仅残存柱础、台阶、神龛底座等遗址。<ref name=":0" />
== 建筑风格与性质 == 由于年代久远、缺乏文字记录，乐山堂的原貌已无从得知。乐山堂遗址占地约1560平方米，从文革末期搭建的简易建筑照片来看，乐山堂依靠立柱支撑，四面并无墙壁。如果这种修筑方式与原建筑相同，那么乐山堂并不适合修行；且乐山堂修建在信徒易为集中的村口，而非适合僧侣修行的偏僻地带，表明乐山堂并不具备[[寺院]]的一般特征，而更符合斋堂的特征。这也是龙首寺更名为乐山堂的可能原因之一。<ref name=":0" />
== 相关活动 == 每年的[[中元节]]（[[农历]]七月十五），上万村的摩尼教信徒会集合在乐山堂举行超度亡人的仪式。这种超度亡灵的理念与崇尚光明的摩尼教教义相左，说明霞浦摩尼教为了在当地大众化、存续至今，已然融合进了一些类似这样超度亡灵的、能慰藉民众的独特内容。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>
== 参见 == * [[霞浦文书]]：记载霞浦摩尼教的重要文献集，是目前发现的篇幅最长的摩尼教汉文文献集，对乐山堂的记载见于文书的《乐山堂神记》。 * [[上万村摩尼教石雕造像]]：修建在乐山堂附近，用于装饰塔后三佛塔和瞪公石塔，是摩尼教存世不多的石雕艺术品。 * [[浦西境福寿宫]]：位于[[福州市]]南门外，是摩尼教保存最完好的宫庙建筑，宫内供奉的是曾在福州救灾的林瞪。
== 参考文献 == <references />{{摩尼教页脚}} [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:966年建立]] [[Category:福建已不存在的建筑物]] [[Category:霞浦县历史]] [[Category:中國已毀地標]] [[Category:宁德宗教史]] [[Category:北宋建筑]] [[Category:北宋宗教]] [[Category:宁德宗教建筑]]

元朝时期，龙首寺改名为乐山堂，明清时期，乐山堂曾两次重修。中华人民共和国成立初期，据当地老人回忆，乐山堂尚有几处院落。[[文化大革命]]初，乐山堂被拆毁，后简单地进行了修缮，搭起了棚式建筑，用于存放村民的农具和临时避雨。2006年，乐山堂毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|超强台风桑美]]，目前仅残存柱础、台阶、神龛底座等遗址。<ref name=":0" />
== 建筑风格与性质 == 由于年代久远、缺乏文字记录，乐山堂的原貌已无从得知。乐山堂遗址占地约1560平方米，从文革末期搭建的简易建筑照片来看，乐山堂依靠立柱支撑，四面并无墙壁。如果这种修筑方式与原建筑相同，那么乐山堂并不适合修行；且乐山堂修建在信徒易为集中的村口，而非适合僧侣修行的偏僻地带，表明乐山堂并不具备[[寺院]]的一般特征，而更符合斋堂的特征。这也是龙首寺更名为乐山堂的可能原因之一。<ref name=":0" />
== 相关活动 == 每年的[[中元节]]（[[农历]]七月十五），上万村的摩尼教信徒会集合在乐山堂举行超度亡人的仪式。这种超度亡灵的理念与崇尚光明的摩尼教教义相左，说明霞浦摩尼教为了在当地大众化、存续至今，已然融合进了一些类似这样超度亡灵的、能慰藉民众的独特内容。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>
== 参见 == * [[霞浦文书]]：记载霞浦摩尼教的重要文献集，是目前发现的篇幅最长的摩尼教汉文文献集，对乐山堂的记载见于文书的《乐山堂神记》。 * [[上万村摩尼教石雕造像]]：修建在乐山堂附近，用于装饰塔后三佛塔和瞪公石塔，是摩尼教存世不多的石雕艺术品。 * [[浦西境福寿宫]]：位于[[福州市]]南门外，是摩尼教保存最完好的宫庙建筑，宫内供奉的是曾在福州救灾的林瞪。
== 参考文献 == <references />{{摩尼教页脚}} [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:966年建立]] [[Category:福建已不存在的建筑物]] [[Category:霞浦县历史]] [[Category:中國已毀地標]] [[Category:宁德宗教史]] [[Category:北宋建筑]] [[Category:北宋宗教]] [[Category:宁德宗教建筑]]

revision.comment: /* 历史沿革 */
revision.text:
{{Not|龙首寺}} {{Coord|27|05|58.6|N|119|54|49.4|E|display=title}} [[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|霞浦县博物馆中展出的乐山堂遗址照片]] '''乐山堂'''，俗称'''盖竹堂'''，旧称'''龙首寺'''，位于中国[[福建省]][[宁德市]][[霞浦县]][[柏洋乡]]塔后村]上万，由福建[[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）教徒孙绵于[[北宋]][[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）建立，是福建境内最早兴建的摩尼教建筑，一千多年来都是柏洋地区的摩尼教重要活动场所，直至2006年毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|桑美台风]]。<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref><ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>
== 名称 == 乐山堂最初建立时，被称作“龙首寺”，元代时改称“乐山堂”。最开始，乐山一词的命名由来被认为是取自《[[论语]]·壅也》当中的“智者乐水，仁者乐山”一句<ref>{{Cite book|title=欧亚学刊 第9辑：第二届传统中国研究国际学术讨论会欧亚专辑|last=Ma|first=Xiaohe|publisher=中华书局|year=2009|location=北京|pages=83-84|chapter=Remains of the Religion of Light in Xiapu County, Fujian Province|authorlink=马小鹤|language=en}}</ref>。后另有说法认为，乐山一词源自[[中古波斯语]]{{Lang|pal|Rōšan}}（或{{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}），意为“光明”<ref>{{Cite book|title=''A Concise Pahlavi Dictionary''|publisher=[[牛津大学出版社|Oxford University Press]]|year=1971|location=[[伦敦|London]]-[[纽约|New York]]-[[多伦多|Toronto]]|pages=72|language=en}}</ref>，与摩尼教崇尚光明的教义相呼应。此外，由于该建筑的性质更可能是教徒活动用的斋堂，而非修道用的寺院（见“建筑风格与性质”章节），故将“寺”更名为“堂”的原因很可能为此。<ref name=":0" />
== 历史沿革 == 据民国21年（1932年）纂修的《民国孙氏宗谱》的明确记载，禅洋村（今又称神洋村，隶属于柏洋乡）人孙绵在北宋[[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）购置此地，并着手修建龙首寺，成为首任寺主。[[天圣]]五年（1027年），上万村人林瞪在[[龙首寺]]皈依明教，后成为当地的明教传教宗师、龙首寺第二代寺主，龙首寺在林瞪的主持下，影响力大大扩增。林瞪之后的寺主则是他的嫡传弟子陈平山。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>
元朝时期，龙首寺改名为乐山堂，明清时期，乐山堂曾两次重修。中华人民共和国成立初期，据当地老人回忆，乐山堂尚有几处院落。[[文化大革命]]初，乐山堂被拆毁，后简单地进行了修缮，搭起了棚式建筑，用于存放村民的农具和临时避雨。2006年，乐山堂毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|超强台风桑美]]，目前仅残存柱础、台阶、神龛底座等遗址。<ref name=":0" />
== 建筑风格与性质 == 由于年代久远、缺乏文字记录，乐山堂的原貌已无从得知。乐山堂遗址占地约1560平方米，从文革末期搭建的简易建筑照片来看，乐山堂依靠立柱支撑，四面并无墙壁。如果这种修筑方式与原建筑相同，那么乐山堂并不适合修行；且乐山堂修建在信徒易为集中的村口，而非适合僧侣修行的偏僻地带，表明乐山堂并不具备[[寺院]]的一般特征，而更符合斋堂的特征。这也是龙首寺更名为乐山堂的可能原因之一。<ref name=":0" />
== 相关活动 == 每年的[[中元节]]（[[农历]]七月十五），上万村的摩尼教信徒会集合在乐山堂举行超度亡人的仪式。这种超度亡灵的理念与崇尚光明的摩尼教教义相左，说明霞浦摩尼教为了在当地大众化、存续至今，已然融合进了一些类似这样超度亡灵的、能慰藉民众的独特内容。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>
== 参见 == * [[霞浦文书]]：记载霞浦摩尼教的重要文献集，是目前发现的篇幅最长的摩尼教汉文文献集，对乐山堂的记载见于文书的《乐山堂神记》。 * [[上万村摩尼教石雕造像]]：修建在乐山堂附近，用于装饰塔后三佛塔和瞪公石塔，是摩尼教存世不多的石雕艺术品。 * [[浦西境福寿宫]]：位于[[福州市]]南门外，是摩尼教保存最完好的宫庙建筑，宫内供奉的是曾在福州救灾的林瞪。
== 参考文献 == <references />{{摩尼教页脚}} [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:966年建立]] [[Category:福建已不存在的建筑物]] [[Category:霞浦县历史]] [[Category:中國已毀地標]] [[Category:宁德宗教史]] [[Category:北宋建筑]] [[Category:北宋宗教]] [[Category:宁德宗教建筑]]

{{Not|龙首寺}} {{Coord|27|05|58.6|N|119|54|49.4|E|display=title}} [[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|霞浦县博物馆中展出的乐山堂遗址照片]] '''乐山堂'''，俗称'''盖竹堂'''，旧称'''龙首寺'''，位于中国[[福建省]][[宁德市]][[霞浦县]][[柏洋乡]][[塔后村 (霞浦县)|塔后村]][[上万村|上万自然村]]，由福建[[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）教徒[[孙绵]]于[[北宋]][[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）建立，是福建境内最早兴建的摩尼教建筑，一千多年来都是柏洋地区的摩尼教重要活动场所，直至2006年毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|桑美台风]]。<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref><ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>
== 名称 == 乐山堂最初建立时，被称作“龙首寺”，元代时改称“乐山堂”。最开始，乐山一词的命名由来被认为是取自《[[论语]]·壅也》当中的“智者乐水，仁者乐山”一句<ref>{{Cite book|title=欧亚学刊 第9辑：第二届传统中国研究国际学术讨论会欧亚专辑|last=Ma|first=Xiaohe|publisher=中华书局|year=2009|location=北京|pages=83-84|chapter=Remains of the Religion of Light in Xiapu County, Fujian Province|authorlink=马小鹤|language=en}}</ref>。后另有说法认为，乐山一词源自[[中古波斯语]]{{Lang|pal|Rōšan}}（或{{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}），意为“光明”<ref>{{Cite book|title=''A Concise Pahlavi Dictionary''|publisher=[[牛津大学出版社|Oxford University Press]]|year=1971|location=[[伦敦|London]]-[[纽约|New York]]-[[多伦多|Toronto]]|pages=72|language=en}}</ref>，与摩尼教崇尚光明的教义相呼应。此外，由于该建筑的性质更可能是教徒活动用的斋堂，而非修道用的寺院（见“建筑风格与性质”章节），故将“寺”更名为“堂”的原因很可能为此。<ref name=":0" />
== 历史沿革 == 据民国21年（1932年）纂修的《民国孙氏宗谱》的明确记载，禅洋村（今又称神洋村，隶属于柏洋乡）人孙绵在北宋[[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）购置此地，并着手修建龙首寺，成为首任寺主。[[天圣]]五年（1027年），上万村人[[林瞪]]在龙首寺皈依明教，后成为当地的明教传教宗师、龙首寺第二代寺主，龙首寺在林瞪的主持下，影响力大大扩增。林瞪之后的寺主则是他的嫡传弟子陈平山。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>
元朝时期，龙首寺改名为乐山堂，明清时期，乐山堂曾两次重修。中华人民共和国成立初期，据当地老人回忆，乐山堂尚有几处院落。[[文化大革命]]初，乐山堂被拆毁，后简单地进行了修缮，搭起了棚式建筑，用于存放村民的农具和临时避雨。2006年，乐山堂毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|超强台风桑美]]，目前仅残存柱础、台阶、神龛底座等遗址。<ref name=":0" />
== 建筑风格与性质 == 由于年代久远、缺乏文字记录，乐山堂的原貌已无从得知。乐山堂遗址占地约1560平方米，从文革末期搭建的简易建筑照片来看，乐山堂依靠立柱支撑，四面并无墙壁。如果这种修筑方式与原建筑相同，那么乐山堂并不适合修行；且乐山堂修建在信徒易为集中的村口，而非适合僧侣修行的偏僻地带，表明乐山堂并不具备[[寺院]]的一般特征，而更符合斋堂的特征。这也是龙首寺更名为乐山堂的可能原因之一。<ref name=":0" />
== 相关活动 == 每年的[[中元节]]（[[农历]]七月十五），上万村的摩尼教信徒会集合在乐山堂举行超度亡人的仪式。这种超度亡灵的理念与崇尚光明的摩尼教教义相左，说明霞浦摩尼教为了在当地大众化、存续至今，已然融合进了一些类似这样超度亡灵的、能慰藉民众的独特内容。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>
== 参见 == * [[霞浦文书]]：记载霞浦摩尼教的重要文献集，是目前发现的篇幅最长的摩尼教汉文文献集，对乐山堂的记载见于文书的《乐山堂神记》。 * [[上万村摩尼教石雕造像]]：修建在乐山堂附近，用于装饰塔后三佛塔和瞪公石塔，是摩尼教存世不多的石雕艺术品。 * [[浦西境福寿宫]]：位于[[福州市]]南门外，是摩尼教保存最完好的宫庙建筑，宫内供奉的是曾在福州救灾的林瞪。
== 参考文献 == <references />{{摩尼教页脚}} [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:966年建立]] [[Category:福建已不存在的建筑物]] [[Category:霞浦县历史]] [[Category:中國已毀地標]] [[Category:宁德宗教史]] [[Category:北宋建筑]] [[Category:北宋宗教]] [[Category:宁德宗教建筑]]

{{Not|龙首寺}} {{Coord|27|05|58.6|N|119|54|49.4|E|display=title}} [[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|霞浦县博物馆中展出的乐山堂遗址照片]] '''乐山堂'''，俗称'''盖竹堂'''，旧称'''龙首寺'''，位于中国[[福建省]][[宁德市]][[霞浦县]][[柏洋乡]][[塔后村 (霞浦县)|塔后村]][[上万村 (霞浦县)|上万自然村]]，由福建[[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）教徒[[孙绵]]于[[北宋]][[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）建立，是福建境内最早兴建的摩尼教建筑，一千多年来都是柏洋地区的摩尼教重要活动场所，直至2006年毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|桑美台风]]。<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref><ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Not|龙首寺}} {{Coord|27|05|58.6|N|119|54|49.4|E|display=title}} [[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|霞浦县博物馆中展出的乐山堂遗址照片]] '''乐山堂'''，俗称'''盖竹堂'''，旧称'''龙首寺'''，位于中国[[福建省]][[宁德市]][[霞浦县]][[柏洋乡]][[塔后村 (霞浦县)|塔后村]][[上万村 (霞浦县)|上万自然村]]，由福建[[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）教徒[[孙绵]]于[[北宋]][[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）建立，是福建境内最早兴建的摩尼教建筑，一千多年来都是柏洋地区的摩尼教重要活动场所，直至2006年毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|桑美台风]]。<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref><ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>
== 名称 == 乐山堂最初建立时，被称作“龙首寺”，元代时改称“乐山堂”。最开始，乐山一词的命名由来被认为是取自《[[论语]]·壅也》当中的“智者乐水，仁者乐山”一句<ref>{{Cite book|title=欧亚学刊 第9辑：第二届传统中国研究国际学术讨论会欧亚专辑|last=Ma|first=Xiaohe|publisher=中华书局|year=2009|location=北京|pages=83-84|chapter=Remains of the Religion of Light in Xiapu County, Fujian Province|authorlink=马小鹤|language=en}}</ref>。后另有说法认为，乐山一词源自[[中古波斯语]]{{Lang|pal|Rōšan}}（或{{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}），意为“光明”<ref>{{Cite book|title=''A Concise Pahlavi Dictionary''|url=https://archive.org/details/concisepahlavidi0000dnma|publisher=[[牛津大学出版社|Oxford University Press]]|year=1971|location=[[伦敦|London]]-[[纽约|New York]]-[[多伦多|Toronto]]|pages=[https://archive.org/details/concisepahlavidi0000dnma/page/72 72]|language=en}}</ref>，与摩尼教崇尚光明的教义相呼应。此外，由于该建筑的性质更可能是教徒活动用的斋堂，而非修道用的寺院（见“建筑风格与性质”章节），故将“寺”更名为“堂”的原因很可能为此。<ref name=":0" />
== 历史沿革 == 据民国21年（1932年）纂修的《民国孙氏宗谱》的明确记载，禅洋村（今又称神洋村，隶属于柏洋乡）人孙绵在北宋[[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）购置此地，并着手修建龙首寺，成为首任寺主。[[天圣]]五年（1027年），上万村人[[林瞪]]在龙首寺皈依明教，后成为当地的明教传教宗师、龙首寺第二代寺主，龙首寺在林瞪的主持下，影响力大大扩增。林瞪之后的寺主则是他的嫡传弟子陈平山。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>
元朝时期，龙首寺改名为乐山堂，明清时期，乐山堂曾两次重修。中华人民共和国成立初期，据当地老人回忆，乐山堂尚有几处院落。[[文化大革命]]初，乐山堂被拆毁，后简单地进行了修缮，搭起了棚式建筑，用于存放村民的农具和临时避雨。2006年，乐山堂毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|超强台风桑美]]，目前仅残存柱础、台阶、神龛底座等遗址。<ref name=":0" />
== 建筑风格与性质 == 由于年代久远、缺乏文字记录，乐山堂的原貌已无从得知。乐山堂遗址占地约1560平方米，从文革末期搭建的简易建筑照片来看，乐山堂依靠立柱支撑，四面并无墙壁。如果这种修筑方式与原建筑相同，那么乐山堂并不适合修行；且乐山堂修建在信徒易为集中的村口，而非适合僧侣修行的偏僻地带，表明乐山堂并不具备[[寺院]]的一般特征，而更符合斋堂的特征。这也是龙首寺更名为乐山堂的可能原因之一。<ref name=":0" />
== 相关活动 == 每年的[[中元节]]（[[农历]]七月十五），上万村的摩尼教信徒会集合在乐山堂举行超度亡人的仪式。这种超度亡灵的理念与崇尚光明的摩尼教教义相左，说明霞浦摩尼教为了在当地大众化、存续至今，已然融合进了一些类似这样超度亡灵的、能慰藉民众的独特内容。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>
== 参见 == * [[霞浦文书]]：记载霞浦摩尼教的重要文献集，是目前发现的篇幅最长的摩尼教汉文文献集，对乐山堂的记载见于文书的《乐山堂神记》。 * [[上万村摩尼教石雕造像]]：修建在乐山堂附近，用于装饰塔后三佛塔和瞪公石塔，是摩尼教存世不多的石雕艺术品。 * [[浦西境福寿宫]]：位于[[福州市]]南门外，是摩尼教保存最完好的宫庙建筑，宫内供奉的是曾在福州救灾的林瞪。
== 参考文献 == <references />{{摩尼教页脚}} [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:966年建立]] [[Category:福建已不存在的建筑物]] [[Category:霞浦县历史]] [[Category:中國已拆除建築物]] [[Category:宁德宗教史]] [[Category:北宋建筑]] [[Category:北宋宗教]] [[Category:宁德宗教建筑]]

== 相关活动 == 每年的[[中元节]]（[[农历]]七月十五），上万村的摩尼教信徒会集合在乐山堂举行超度亡人的仪式。这种超度亡灵的理念与崇尚光明的摩尼教教义相左，说明霞浦摩尼教为了在当地大众化、存续至今，已然融合进了一些类似这样超度亡灵的、能慰藉民众的独特内容。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>
== 参见 == * [[霞浦文书]]：记载霞浦摩尼教的重要文献集，是目前发现的篇幅最长的摩尼教汉文文献集，对乐山堂的记载见于文书的《乐山堂神记》。 * [[上万村摩尼教石雕造像]]：修建在乐山堂附近，用于装饰塔后三佛塔和瞪公石塔，是摩尼教存世不多的石雕艺术品。 * [[浦西境福寿宫]]：位于[[福州市]]南门外，是摩尼教保存最完好的宫庙建筑，宫内供奉的是曾在福州救灾的林瞪。
== 参考文献 == <references />{{摩尼教页脚}} [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:966年建立]] [[Category:福建已不存在的建筑物]] [[Category:霞浦县历史]] [[Category:中國已拆除建築物]] [[Category:宁德宗教史]] [[Category:北宋宗教建筑]] [[Category:宁德宗教建筑]]

revision.comment: 机器人: 尚未清空的已重定向分类） （[[Cat:宁德宗教建筑]]→[[Cat:宁德市宗教建筑物]]
revision.text:
{{Not|龙首寺}} {{Coord|27|05|58.6|N|119|54|49.4|E|display=title}} [[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|霞浦县博物馆中展出的乐山堂遗址照片]] '''乐山堂'''，俗称'''盖竹堂'''，旧称'''龙首寺'''，位于中国[[福建省]][[宁德市]][[霞浦县]][[柏洋乡]][[塔后村 (霞浦县)|塔后村]][[上万村 (霞浦县)|上万自然村]]，由福建[[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）教徒[[孙绵]]于[[北宋]][[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）建立，是福建境内最早兴建的摩尼教建筑，一千多年来都是柏洋地区的摩尼教重要活动场所，直至2006年毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|桑美台风]]。<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref><ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>
== 名称 == 乐山堂最初建立时，被称作“龙首寺”，元代时改称“乐山堂”。最开始，乐山一词的命名由来被认为是取自《[[论语]]·壅也》当中的“智者乐水，仁者乐山”一句<ref>{{Cite book|title=欧亚学刊 第9辑：第二届传统中国研究国际学术讨论会欧亚专辑|last=Ma|first=Xiaohe|publisher=中华书局|year=2009|location=北京|pages=83-84|chapter=Remains of the Religion of Light in Xiapu County, Fujian Province|authorlink=马小鹤|language=en}}</ref>。后另有说法认为，乐山一词源自[[中古波斯语]]{{Lang|pal|Rōšan}}（或{{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}），意为“光明”<ref>{{Cite book|title=''A Concise Pahlavi Dictionary''|url=https://archive.org/details/concisepahlavidi0000dnma|publisher=[[牛津大学出版社|Oxford University Press]]|year=1971|location=[[伦敦|London]]-[[纽约|New York]]-[[多伦多|Toronto]]|pages=[https://archive.org/details/concisepahlavidi0000dnma/page/72 72]|language=en}}</ref>，与摩尼教崇尚光明的教义相呼应。此外，由于该建筑的性质更可能是教徒活动用的斋堂，而非修道用的寺院（见“建筑风格与性质”章节），故将“寺”更名为“堂”的原因很可能为此。<ref name=":0" />
== 历史沿革 == 据民国21年（1932年）纂修的《民国孙氏宗谱》的明确记载，禅洋村（今又称神洋村，隶属于柏洋乡）人孙绵在北宋[[乾德 (北宋)|乾德]]四年（966年）购置此地，并着手修建龙首寺，成为首任寺主。[[天圣]]五年（1027年），上万村人[[林瞪]]在龙首寺皈依明教，后成为当地的明教传教宗师、龙首寺第二代寺主，龙首寺在林瞪的主持下，影响力大大扩增。林瞪之后的寺主则是他的嫡传弟子陈平山。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>
元朝时期，龙首寺改名为乐山堂，明清时期，乐山堂曾两次重修。中华人民共和国成立初期，据当地老人回忆，乐山堂尚有几处院落。[[文化大革命]]初，乐山堂被拆毁，后简单地进行了修缮，搭起了棚式建筑，用于存放村民的农具和临时避雨。2006年，乐山堂毁于[[颱風桑美 (2006年)|超强台风桑美]]，目前仅残存柱础、台阶、神龛底座等遗址。<ref name=":0" />
== 建筑风格与性质 == 由于年代久远、缺乏文字记录，乐山堂的原貌已无从得知。乐山堂遗址占地约1560平方米，从文革末期搭建的简易建筑照片来看，乐山堂依靠立柱支撑，四面并无墙壁。如果这种修筑方式与原建筑相同，那么乐山堂并不适合修行；且乐山堂修建在信徒易为集中的村口，而非适合僧侣修行的偏僻地带，表明乐山堂并不具备[[寺院]]的一般特征，而更符合斋堂的特征。这也是龙首寺更名为乐山堂的可能原因之一。<ref name=":0" />
== 相关活动 == 每年的[[中元节]]（[[农历]]七月十五），上万村的摩尼教信徒会集合在乐山堂举行超度亡人的仪式。这种超度亡灵的理念与崇尚光明的摩尼教教义相左，说明霞浦摩尼教为了在当地大众化、存续至今，已然融合进了一些类似这样超度亡灵的、能慰藉民众的独特内容。<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>
== 参见 == * [[霞浦文书]]：记载霞浦摩尼教的重要文献集，是目前发现的篇幅最长的摩尼教汉文文献集，对乐山堂的记载见于文书的《乐山堂神记》。 * [[上万村摩尼教石雕造像]]：修建在乐山堂附近，用于装饰塔后三佛塔和瞪公石塔，是摩尼教存世不多的石雕艺术品。 * [[浦西境福寿宫]]：位于[[福州市]]南门外，是摩尼教保存最完好的宫庙建筑，宫内供奉的是曾在福州救灾的林瞪。
== 参考文献 == <references />{{摩尼教页脚}} [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:966年建立]] [[Category:福建已不存在的建筑物]] [[Category:霞浦县历史]] [[Category:中國已拆除建築物]] [[Category:宁德宗教史]] [[Category:北宋宗教建筑]] [[Category:宁德市宗教建筑物]]

{{Not|Longshou Temple}}
{{Coord|27|05|58.6|N|119|54|49.4|E|display=title}}
[[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|Photo of the Leshan Hall ruins exhibited at the [[:en:Xiapu|Xiapu County]] Museum]]

'''Leshan Hall''', commonly known as '''Gaizhu Hall''', formerly known as '''Longshou Temple''', is located in China, [[:en:Fujian|Fujian Province]], [[:en:Ningde|Ningde City]], [[:en:Xiapu|Xiapu County]], {{ill|Baiyang Township|de|Baiyang Township|ja|柏洋郷|zh|柏洋乡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baiyang Township|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tahu Village (Xiapu County)|de|Tahu-Dorf (Xiapu County)|ja|塔後村 (霞浦県)|zh|塔后村|lt=Tahu Village|lt_ja=塔後村|lt_de=Tahu-Dorf|lt_zh=塔后村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahu Village (Xiapu County)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shangwan Village (Xiapu County)|de|Shangwan-Dorf (Xiapu County)|ja|上万村 (霞浦県)|zh|上万村|lt=Shangwan Natural Village|lt_ja=上万自然村|lt_de=Shangwan-Naturdorf|lt_zh=上万自然村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shangwan Village (Xiapu County)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established by Fujian {{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mingjiao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐代三夷教|1=Three Persian religions in Tang dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418516}} ([[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]]) follower {{ill|Sun Mian|de|Sun Mian|ja|孫綿|zh|孙绵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Mian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the {{ill|Northern Song|de|Nördliche Song-Dynastie|ja|北宋|zh|北宋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Song|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Northern Song dynasty|WD=Q319460}} period in the fourth year of {{ill|Qiande (Northern Song)|de|Qiande (Northern Song)|ja|乾德 (北宋)|zh|乾德|lt=Qiande|lt_ja=乾德|lt_de=Qiande|lt_zh=乾德|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qiande (Northern Song)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (966 AD), making it the earliest Manichaean building constructed in Fujian. For over a thousand years, it served as an important venue for Manichaean activities in the Baiyang area until it was destroyed in 2006 by {{ill|Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|de|Taifun Sangmei (2006)|ja|颱風桑美 (2006年)|zh|颱風桑美|lt=Sangmei Typhoon|lt_ja=桑美台風|lt_de=Sangmei-Taifun|lt_zh=桑美台风|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref>
<ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>

== Name ==
Leshan Hall was originally established as "Longshou Temple" and was renamed "Leshan Hall" during the Yuan dynasty. Initially, the name "Leshan" was believed to be derived from the phrase "The wise delight in water, and the benevolent delight in mountains" found in [[:en:Analects|The Analects]] (Lunyu: Yong Ye). Another theory suggests that the term "Leshan" originates from {{ill|Middle Persian|de|Mittelpersisch|ja|中古ペルシア語|zh|中古波斯语|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Persian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Lang|pal|Rōšan}} (or {{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}), meaning "light", which corresponds with the Manichaean doctrine that venerates light. Moreover, since the building was more likely used as a dining hall for followers rather than as a monastery (see the "Architectural Style and Nature" section), the change from "Temple" (寺) to "Hall" (堂) was probably due to this reason.
<ref name=":0" />

== Historical Development ==
According to the explicit records in the "Sun Family Genealogy of the Republic of China" compiled in the 21st year of the Republic (1932), Sun Mian from Chanyang Village (now also known as Shenyang Village, under the jurisdiction of Baiyang Township) purchased the land during the fourth year of {{ill|Qiande (Northern Song)|de|Qiande (Northern Song)|ja|乾德 (北宋)|zh|乾德|lt=Qiande|lt_ja=乾德|lt_de=Qiande|lt_zh=乾德|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qiande (Northern Song)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (966 AD) and commenced the construction of Longshou Temple, becoming its first abbot. In the fifth year of {{ill|Tiansheng|de|Tiansheng|ja|天聖|zh|天圣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiansheng|qq=|1=Tiansheng|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939230}} (1027 AD), a resident of Shangwan Village, {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, converted to Mingjiao at Longshou Temple, later becoming the leading missionary master of Mingjiao in the area and the second abbot of Longshou Temple. His direct disciple, Chen Pingshan, succeeded him.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>

During the Yuan dynasty, Longshou Temple was renamed Leshan Hall, and during the Ming and Qing dynasties, Leshan Hall underwent two major renovations. In the early years of the People's Republic of China, according to local elders, Leshan Hall still had several courtyards. At the beginning of the [[:en:Cultural Revolution|Cultural Revolution]], Leshan Hall was demolished and subsequently received only simple repairs with makeshift shed-like structures used for storing villagers' farming tools and for temporary shelter from rain. In 2006, Leshan Hall was destroyed by the {{ill|Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|de|Taifun Sangmei (2006)|ja|颱風桑美 (2006年)|zh|颱風桑美|lt=Super Typhoon Sangmei|lt_ja=超強台風桑美|lt_de=Super-Taifun Sangmei|lt_zh=超强台风桑美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leaving only the foundation columns, steps, and the base of the shrine.
<ref name=":0" />

== Architectural Style and Nature ==
Due to its great antiquity and the lack of written records, the original appearance of Leshan Hall is unknown. The site covers an area of approximately 1560 square meters. Based on photographs of the makeshift structure built at the end of the Cultural Revolution, Leshan Hall relied on columns for support and had no walls on any side. If this construction method was similar to the original, then Leshan Hall would not have been suitable for monastic practice; furthermore, its location at the village entrance, where followers tended to gather, rather than in a secluded area suitable for monks, indicates that Leshan Hall did not possess the typical characteristics of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Temple]], but rather those of a dining hall. This is one possible reason for the renaming from Longshou Temple to Leshan Hall.
<ref name=":0" />

== Related Activities ==
Every year on the {{ill|Ghost Festival|de|Geisterfest|ja|中元節|zh|中元节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ghost Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中元|1=Ulambana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q696781}} (the fifteenth day of the seventh month of the {{ill|Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender|ja|農暦|zh|农历|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国暦|1=Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134032}}), the Manichaean believers of Shangwan Village gather at Leshan Hall to perform a ritual for the transference of the souls of the deceased. This concept of soul transference is at odds with the Manichaean doctrine that venerates light, indicating that Manichaeism in Xiapu, in order to become popular and endure locally, had incorporated some unique elements—such as this soul-transference ritual—that provided comfort to the people.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu-Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu Documents|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  A collection of important documents on Manichaeism in Xiapu, it is currently the longest extant collection of Manichaean Chinese texts, with an account of Leshan Hall found in the manuscript "Leshan Hall Divine Record".
* {{ill|Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|de|Manichäische Steinskulpturen aus Shangwan Village|ja|上万村摩尼教石彫造像|zh|上万村摩尼教石雕造像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Built near Leshan Hall to adorn the Three Buddha Pagoda behind the tower and the Dengdong Stone Pagoda, it is one of the few surviving examples of Manichaean stone carving art.
* {{ill|Fushou Palace in Puxi|de|Fushou-Palast in Puxi|ja|浦西境福寿宮|zh|浦西境福寿宫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushou Palace in Puxi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Located outside the south gate of [[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou City]], it is the best-preserved Manichaean palace building, enshrining Lin Deng who once aided in disaster relief in Fuzhou.

{{Coord|27|05|58.6|N|119|54|49.4|E|display=title}}
[[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|Photo of the Leshan Hall ruins exhibited at the [[:en:Xiapu|Xiapu County]] Museum]]

'''Leshan Hall''', commonly known as '''Gaizhu Hall''', formerly known as '''Longshou Temple''', is located in China, [[:en:Fujian|Fujian Province]], [[:en:Ningde|Ningde City]], [[:en:Xiapu|Xiapu County]], {{ill|Baiyang Township|de|Baiyang Township|ja|柏洋郷|zh|柏洋乡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baiyang Township|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tahu Village (Xiapu County)|de|Tahu-Dorf (Xiapu County)|ja|塔後村 (霞浦県)|zh|塔后村|lt=Tahu Village|lt_ja=塔後村|lt_de=Tahu-Dorf|lt_zh=塔后村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahu Village (Xiapu County)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shangwan Village (Xiapu County)|de|Shangwan-Dorf (Xiapu County)|ja|上万村 (霞浦県)|zh|上万村|lt=Shangwan Natural Village|lt_ja=上万自然村|lt_de=Shangwan-Naturdorf|lt_zh=上万自然村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shangwan Village (Xiapu County)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established by Fujian {{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mingjiao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐代三夷教|1=Three Persian religions in Tang dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418516}} ([[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]]) follower {{ill|Sun Mian|de|Sun Mian|ja|孫綿|zh|孙绵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Mian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the {{ill|Northern Song|de|Nördliche Song-Dynastie|ja|北宋|zh|北宋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Song|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Northern Song dynasty|WD=Q319460}} period in the fourth year of {{ill|Qiande (Northern Song)|de|Qiande (Northern Song)|ja|乾德 (北宋)|zh|乾德|lt=Qiande|lt_ja=乾德|lt_de=Qiande|lt_zh=乾德|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qiande (Northern Song)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (966 AD), making it the earliest Manichaean building constructed in Fujian. For over a thousand years, it served as an important venue for Manichaean activities in the Baiyang area until it was destroyed in 2006 by {{ill|Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|de|Taifun Sangmei (2006)|ja|颱風桑美 (2006年)|zh|颱風桑美|lt=Sangmei Typhoon|lt_ja=桑美台風|lt_de=Sangmei-Taifun|lt_zh=桑美台风|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref>
<ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>

== Name ==
Leshan Hall was originally established as "Longshou Temple" and was renamed "Leshan Hall" during the Yuan dynasty. Initially, the name "Leshan" was believed to be derived from the phrase "The wise delight in water, and the benevolent delight in mountains" found in [[:en:Analects|The Analects]] (Lunyu: Yong Ye). Another theory suggests that the term "Leshan" originates from {{ill|Middle Persian|de|Mittelpersisch|ja|中古ペルシア語|zh|中古波斯语|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Persian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Lang|pal|Rōšan}} (or {{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}), meaning "light", which corresponds with the Manichaean doctrine that venerates light. Moreover, since the building was more likely used as a dining hall for followers rather than as a monastery (see the "Architectural Style and Nature" section), the change from "Temple" (寺) to "Hall" (堂) was probably due to this reason.
<ref name=":0" />

== Historical Development ==
According to the explicit records in the "Sun Family Genealogy of the Republic of China" compiled in the 21st year of the Republic (1932), Sun Mian from Chanyang Village (now also known as Shenyang Village, under the jurisdiction of Baiyang Township) purchased the land during the fourth year of {{ill|Qiande (Northern Song)|de|Qiande (Northern Song)|ja|乾德 (北宋)|zh|乾德|lt=Qiande|lt_ja=乾德|lt_de=Qiande|lt_zh=乾德|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qiande (Northern Song)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (966 AD) and commenced the construction of Longshou Temple, becoming its first abbot. In the fifth year of {{ill|Tiansheng|de|Tiansheng|ja|天聖|zh|天圣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiansheng|qq=|1=Tiansheng|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939230}} (1027 AD), a resident of Shangwan Village, {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, converted to Mingjiao at Longshou Temple, later becoming the leading missionary master of Mingjiao in the area and the second abbot of Longshou Temple. His direct disciple, Chen Pingshan, succeeded him.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>

During the Yuan dynasty, Longshou Temple was renamed Leshan Hall, and during the Ming and Qing dynasties, Leshan Hall underwent two major renovations. In the early years of the People's Republic of China, according to local elders, Leshan Hall still had several courtyards. At the beginning of the [[:en:Cultural Revolution|Cultural Revolution]], Leshan Hall was demolished and subsequently received only simple repairs with makeshift shed-like structures used for storing villagers' farming tools and for temporary shelter from rain. In 2006, Leshan Hall was destroyed by the {{ill|Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|de|Taifun Sangmei (2006)|ja|颱風桑美 (2006年)|zh|颱風桑美|lt=Super Typhoon Sangmei|lt_ja=超強台風桑美|lt_de=Super-Taifun Sangmei|lt_zh=超强台风桑美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leaving only the foundation columns, steps, and the base of the shrine.
<ref name=":0" />

== Architectural Style and Nature ==
Due to its great antiquity and the lack of written records, the original appearance of Leshan Hall is unknown. The site covers an area of approximately 1560 square meters. Based on photographs of the makeshift structure built at the end of the Cultural Revolution, Leshan Hall relied on columns for support and had no walls on any side. If this construction method was similar to the original, then Leshan Hall would not have been suitable for monastic practice; furthermore, its location at the village entrance, where followers tended to gather, rather than in a secluded area suitable for monks, indicates that Leshan Hall did not possess the typical characteristics of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Temple]], but rather those of a dining hall. This is one possible reason for the renaming from Longshou Temple to Leshan Hall.
<ref name=":0" />

== Related Activities ==
Every year on the {{ill|Ghost Festival|de|Geisterfest|ja|中元節|zh|中元节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ghost Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中元|1=Ulambana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q696781}} (the fifteenth day of the seventh month of the {{ill|Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender|ja|農暦|zh|农历|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国暦|1=Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134032}}), the Manichaean believers of Shangwan Village gather at Leshan Hall to perform a ritual for the transference of the souls of the deceased. This concept of soul transference is at odds with the Manichaean doctrine that venerates light, indicating that Manichaeism in Xiapu, in order to become popular and endure locally, had incorporated some unique elements—such as this soul-transference ritual—that provided comfort to the people.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>

  A collection of important documents on Manichaeism in Xiapu, it is currently the longest extant collection of Manichaean Chinese texts, with an account of Leshan Hall found in the manuscript "Leshan Hall Divine Record".
* {{ill|Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|de|Manichäische Steinskulpturen aus Shangwan Village|ja|上万村摩尼教石彫造像|zh|上万村摩尼教石雕造像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Built near Leshan Hall to adorn the Three Buddha Pagoda behind the tower and the Dengdong Stone Pagoda, it is one of the few surviving examples of Manichaean stone carving art.
* {{ill|Fushou Palace in Puxi|de|Fushou-Palast in Puxi|ja|浦西境福寿宮|zh|浦西境福寿宫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushou Palace in Puxi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Located outside the south gate of [[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou City]], it is the best-preserved Manichaean palace building, enshrining Lin Deng who once aided in disaster relief in Fuzhou.

[[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|Photo of the Leshan Hall ruins exhibited at the [[:en:Xiapu|Xiapu County]] Museum]]

'''Leshan Hall''', commonly known as '''Gaizhu Hall''', formerly known as '''Longshou Temple''', is located in China, [[:en:Fujian|Fujian Province]], [[:en:Ningde|Ningde City]], [[:en:Xiapu|Xiapu County]], {{ill|Baiyang Township|de|Baiyang Township|ja|柏洋郷|zh|柏洋乡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baiyang Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tahu Village (Xiapu County)|de|Tahu-Dorf (Xiapu County)|ja|塔後村 (霞浦県)|zh|塔后村|lt=Tahu Village|lt_ja=塔後村|lt_de=Tahu-Dorf|lt_zh=塔后村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahu Village (Xiapu County)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shangwan Village (Xiapu County)|de|Shangwan-Dorf (Xiapu County)|ja|上万村 (霞浦県)|zh|上万村|lt=Shangwan Natural Village|lt_ja=上万自然村|lt_de=Shangwan-Naturdorf|lt_zh=上万自然村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shangwan Village (Xiapu County)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established by Fujian {{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mingjiao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐代三夷教|1=Three Persian religions in Tang dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418516}} ([[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]]) follower {{ill|Sun Mian|de|Sun Mian|ja|孫綿|zh|孙绵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Mian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the {{ill|Northern Song|de|Nördliche Song-Dynastie|ja|北宋|zh|北宋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Song|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Northern Song dynasty|WD=Q319460}} period in the fourth year of {{ill|Qiande (Northern Song)|de|Qiande (Northern Song)|ja|乾德 (北宋)|zh|乾德|lt=Qiande|lt_ja=乾德|lt_de=Qiande|lt_zh=乾德|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qiande (Northern Song)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (966 AD), making it the earliest Manichaean building constructed in Fujian. For over a thousand years, it served as an important venue for Manichaean activities in the Baiyang area until it was destroyed in 2006 by {{ill|Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|de|Taifun Sangmei (2006)|ja|颱風桑美 (2006年)|zh|颱風桑美|lt=Sangmei Typhoon|lt_ja=桑美台風|lt_de=Sangmei-Taifun|lt_zh=桑美台风|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref>
<ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>

== Name ==
Leshan Hall was originally established as "Longshou Temple" and was renamed "Leshan Hall" during the Yuan dynasty. Initially, the name "Leshan" was believed to be derived from the phrase "The wise delight in water, and the benevolent delight in mountains" found in [[:en:Analects|The Analects]] (Lunyu: Yong Ye). Another theory suggests that the term "Leshan" originates from {{ill|Middle Persian|de|Mittelpersisch|ja|中古ペルシア語|zh|中古波斯语|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Persian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Lang|pal|Rōšan}} (or {{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}), meaning "light", which corresponds with the Manichaean doctrine that venerates light. Moreover, since the building was more likely used as a dining hall for followers rather than as a monastery (see the "Architectural Style and Nature" section), the change from "Temple" (寺) to "Hall" (堂) was probably due to this reason.
<ref name=":0" />

== Historical Development ==
According to the explicit records in the "Sun Family Genealogy of the Republic of China" compiled in the 21st year of the Republic (1932), Sun Mian from Chanyang Village (now also known as Shenyang Village, under the jurisdiction of Baiyang Township) purchased the land during the fourth year of {{ill|Qiande (Northern Song)|de|Qiande (Northern Song)|ja|乾德 (北宋)|zh|乾德|lt=Qiande|lt_ja=乾德|lt_de=Qiande|lt_zh=乾德|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qiande (Northern Song)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (966 AD) and commenced the construction of Longshou Temple, becoming its first abbot. In the fifth year of {{ill|Tiansheng|de|Tiansheng|ja|天聖|zh|天圣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiansheng|1=Tiansheng|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939230}} (1027 AD), a resident of Shangwan Village, {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, converted to Mingjiao at Longshou Temple, later becoming the leading missionary master of Mingjiao in the area and the second abbot of Longshou Temple. His direct disciple, Chen Pingshan, succeeded him.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>

During the Yuan dynasty, Longshou Temple was renamed Leshan Hall, and during the Ming and Qing dynasties, Leshan Hall underwent two major renovations. In the early years of the People's Republic of China, according to local elders, Leshan Hall still had several courtyards. At the beginning of the [[:en:Cultural Revolution|Cultural Revolution]], Leshan Hall was demolished and subsequently received only simple repairs with makeshift shed-like structures used for storing villagers' farming tools and for temporary shelter from rain. In 2006, Leshan Hall was destroyed by the {{ill|Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|de|Taifun Sangmei (2006)|ja|颱風桑美 (2006年)|zh|颱風桑美|lt=Super Typhoon Sangmei|lt_ja=超強台風桑美|lt_de=Super-Taifun Sangmei|lt_zh=超强台风桑美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leaving only the foundation columns, steps, and the base of the shrine.
<ref name=":0" />

== Architectural Style and Nature ==
Due to its great antiquity and the lack of written records, the original appearance of Leshan Hall is unknown. The site covers an area of approximately 1560 square meters. Based on photographs of the makeshift structure built at the end of the Cultural Revolution, Leshan Hall relied on columns for support and had no walls on any side. If this construction method was similar to the original, then Leshan Hall would not have been suitable for monastic practice; furthermore, its location at the village entrance, where followers tended to gather, rather than in a secluded area suitable for monks, indicates that Leshan Hall did not possess the typical characteristics of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Temple]], but rather those of a dining hall. This is one possible reason for the renaming from Longshou Temple to Leshan Hall.
<ref name=":0" />

== Related Activities ==
Every year on the {{ill|Ghost Festival|de|Geisterfest|ja|中元節|zh|中元节|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ghost Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中元|1=Ulambana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q696781}} (the fifteenth day of the seventh month of the {{ill|Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender|ja|農暦|zh|农历|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国暦|1=Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134032}}), the Manichaean believers of Shangwan Village gather at Leshan Hall to perform a ritual for the transference of the souls of the deceased. This concept of soul transference is at odds with the Manichaean doctrine that venerates light, indicating that Manichaeism in Xiapu, in order to become popular and endure locally, had incorporated some unique elements—such as this soul-transference ritual—that provided comfort to the people.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu-Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  A collection of important documents on Manichaeism in Xiapu, it is currently the longest extant collection of Manichaean Chinese texts, with an account of Leshan Hall found in the manuscript "Leshan Hall Divine Record".
* {{ill|Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|de|Manichäische Steinskulpturen aus Shangwan Village|ja|上万村摩尼教石彫造像|zh|上万村摩尼教石雕造像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Built near Leshan Hall to adorn the Three Buddha Pagoda behind the tower and the Dengdong Stone Pagoda, it is one of the few surviving examples of Manichaean stone carving art.
* {{ill|Fushou Palace in Puxi|de|Fushou-Palast in Puxi|ja|浦西境福寿宮|zh|浦西境福寿宫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushou Palace in Puxi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Located outside the south gate of [[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou City]], it is the best-preserved Manichaean palace building, enshrining Lin Deng who once aided in disaster relief in Fuzhou.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[zh:乐山堂]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none)
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none)
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:21, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none)
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Not|Longshou Temple}}
{{Coord|27|05|58.6|N|119|54|49.4|E|display=title}}
[[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|Photo of the Leshan Hall ruins exhibited at the [[:en:Xiapu|Xiapu County]] Museum]]

'''Leshan Hall''', commonly known as '''Gaizhu Hall''', formerly known as '''Longshou Temple''', is located in China, [[:en:Fujian|Fujian Province]], [[:en:Ningde|Ningde City]], [[:en:Xiapu|Xiapu County]], {{ill|Baiyang Township|de|Baiyang Township|ja|柏洋郷|zh|柏洋乡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baiyang Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tahu Village (Xiapu County)|de|Tahu-Dorf (Xiapu County)|ja|塔後村 (霞浦県)|zh|塔后村|lt=Tahu Village|lt_ja=塔後村|lt_de=Tahu-Dorf|lt_zh=塔后村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahu Village (Xiapu County)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shangwan Village (Xiapu County)|de|Shangwan-Dorf (Xiapu County)|ja|上万村 (霞浦県)|zh|上万村|lt=Shangwan Natural Village|lt_ja=上万自然村|lt_de=Shangwan-Naturdorf|lt_zh=上万自然村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shangwan Village (Xiapu County)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established by Fujian {{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mingjiao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐代三夷教|1=Three Persian religions in Tang dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418516}} ([[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]]) follower {{ill|Sun Mian|de|Sun Mian|ja|孫綿|zh|孙绵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Mian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the {{ill|Northern Song|de|Nördliche Song-Dynastie|ja|北宋|zh|北宋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Song|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Northern Song dynasty|WD=Q319460}} period in the fourth year of {{ill|Qiande (Northern Song)|de|Qiande (Northern Song)|ja|乾德 (北宋)|zh|乾德|lt=Qiande|lt_ja=乾德|lt_de=Qiande|lt_zh=乾德|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qiande (Northern Song)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (966 AD), making it the earliest Manichaean building constructed in Fujian. For over a thousand years, it served as an important venue for Manichaean activities in the Baiyang area until it was destroyed in 2006 by {{ill|Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|de|Taifun Sangmei (2006)|ja|颱風桑美 (2006年)|zh|颱風桑美|lt=Sangmei Typhoon|lt_ja=桑美台風|lt_de=Sangmei-Taifun|lt_zh=桑美台风|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref>
<ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>

== Name ==
Leshan Hall was originally established as "Longshou Temple" and was renamed "Leshan Hall" during the Yuan dynasty. Initially, the name "Leshan" was believed to be derived from the phrase "The wise delight in water, and the benevolent delight in mountains" found in [[:en:Analects|The Analects]] (Lunyu: Yong Ye). Another theory suggests that the term "Leshan" originates from {{ill|Middle Persian|de|Mittelpersisch|ja|中古ペルシア語|zh|中古波斯语|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Persian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Lang|pal|Rōšan}} (or {{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}), meaning "light", which corresponds with the Manichaean doctrine that venerates light. Moreover, since the building was more likely used as a dining hall for followers rather than as a monastery (see the "Architectural Style and Nature" section), the change from "Temple" (寺) to "Hall" (堂) was probably due to this reason.
<ref name=":0" />

== Historical Development ==
According to the explicit records in the "Sun Family Genealogy of the Republic of China" compiled in the 21st year of the Republic (1932), Sun Mian from Chanyang Village (now also known as Shenyang Village, under the jurisdiction of Baiyang Township) purchased the land during the fourth year of {{ill|Qiande (Northern Song)|de|Qiande (Northern Song)|ja|乾德 (北宋)|zh|乾德|lt=Qiande|lt_ja=乾德|lt_de=Qiande|lt_zh=乾德|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qiande (Northern Song)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (966 AD) and commenced the construction of Longshou Temple, becoming its first abbot. In the fifth year of {{ill|Tiansheng|de|Tiansheng|ja|天聖|zh|天圣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiansheng|1=Tiansheng|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939230}} (1027 AD), a resident of Shangwan Village, {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, converted to Mingjiao at Longshou Temple, later becoming the leading missionary master of Mingjiao in the area and the second abbot of Longshou Temple. His direct disciple, Chen Pingshan, succeeded him.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>

During the Yuan dynasty, Longshou Temple was renamed Leshan Hall, and during the Ming and Qing dynasties, Leshan Hall underwent two major renovations. In the early years of the People's Republic of China, according to local elders, Leshan Hall still had several courtyards. At the beginning of the [[:en:Cultural Revolution|Cultural Revolution]], Leshan Hall was demolished and subsequently received only simple repairs with makeshift shed-like structures used for storing villagers' farming tools and for temporary shelter from rain. In 2006, Leshan Hall was destroyed by the {{ill|Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|de|Taifun Sangmei (2006)|ja|颱風桑美 (2006年)|zh|颱風桑美|lt=Super Typhoon Sangmei|lt_ja=超強台風桑美|lt_de=Super-Taifun Sangmei|lt_zh=超强台风桑美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leaving only the foundation columns, steps, and the base of the shrine.
<ref name=":0" />

== Architectural Style and Nature ==
Due to its great antiquity and the lack of written records, the original appearance of Leshan Hall is unknown. The site covers an area of approximately 1560 square meters. Based on photographs of the makeshift structure built at the end of the Cultural Revolution, Leshan Hall relied on columns for support and had no walls on any side. If this construction method was similar to the original, then Leshan Hall would not have been suitable for monastic practice; furthermore, its location at the village entrance, where followers tended to gather, rather than in a secluded area suitable for monks, indicates that Leshan Hall did not possess the typical characteristics of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Temple]], but rather those of a dining hall. This is one possible reason for the renaming from Longshou Temple to Leshan Hall.
<ref name=":0" />

== Related Activities ==
Every year on the {{ill|Ghost Festival|de|Geisterfest|ja|中元節|zh|中元节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ghost Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中元|1=Ulambana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q696781}} (the fifteenth day of the seventh month of the {{ill|Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender|ja|農暦|zh|农历|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国暦|1=Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134032}}), the Manichaean believers of Shangwan Village gather at Leshan Hall to perform a ritual for the transference of the souls of the deceased. This concept of soul transference is at odds with the Manichaean doctrine that venerates light, indicating that Manichaeism in Xiapu, in order to become popular and endure locally, had incorporated some unique elements—such as this soul-transference ritual—that provided comfort to the people.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu-Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  A collection of important documents on Manichaeism in Xiapu, it is currently the longest extant collection of Manichaean Chinese texts, with an account of Leshan Hall found in the manuscript "Leshan Hall Divine Record".
* {{ill|Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|de|Manichäische Steinskulpturen aus Shangwan Village|ja|上万村摩尼教石彫造像|zh|上万村摩尼教石雕造像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Built near Leshan Hall to adorn the Three Buddha Pagoda behind the tower and the Dengdong Stone Pagoda, it is one of the few surviving examples of Manichaean stone carving art.
* {{ill|Fushou Palace in Puxi|de|Fushou-Palast in Puxi|ja|浦西境福寿宮|zh|浦西境福寿宫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushou Palace in Puxi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Located outside the south gate of [[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou City]], it is the best-preserved Manichaean palace building, enshrining Lin Deng who once aided in disaster relief in Fuzhou.

Every year on the {{ill|Ghost Festival|de|Geisterfest|ja|中元節|zh|中元节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ghost Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中元|1=Ulambana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q696781}} (the fifteenth day of the seventh month of the {{ill|Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender|ja|農暦|zh|农历|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国暦|1=Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134032}}), the Manichaean believers of Shangwan Village gather at Leshan Hall to perform a ritual for the transference of the souls of the deceased. <ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu-Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  A collection of important documents on Manichaeism in Xiapu, it is currently the longest extant collection of Manichaean Chinese texts, with an account of Leshan Hall found in the manuscript "Leshan Hall Divine Record".
* {{ill|Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|de|Manichäische Steinskulpturen aus Shangwan Village|ja|上万村摩尼教石彫造像|zh|上万村摩尼教石雕造像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Built near Leshan Hall to adorn the Three Buddha Pagoda behind the tower and the Dengdong Stone Pagoda, it is one of the few surviving examples of Manichaean stone carving art.
* {{ill|Fushou Palace in Puxi|de|Fushou-Palast in Puxi|ja|浦西境福寿宮|zh|浦西境福寿宫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushou Palace in Puxi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Located outside the south gate of [[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou City]], it is the best-preserved Manichaean palace building, enshrining Lin Deng who once aided in disaster relief in Fuzhou.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[zh:乐山堂]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none)
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none)

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Not|Longshou Temple}}
{{Coord|27|05|58.6|N|119|54|49.4|E|display=title}}
[[File:泗洲文佛像和乐山堂遗址.jpg|thumb|Photo of the Leshan Hall ruins exhibited at the [[:en:Xiapu|Xiapu County]] Museum]]

'''Leshan Hall''', commonly known as '''Gaizhu Hall''', formerly known as '''Longshou Temple''', is located in China, [[:en:Fujian|Fujian Province]], [[:en:Ningde|Ningde City]], [[:en:Xiapu|Xiapu County]], {{ill|Baiyang Township|de|Baiyang Township|ja|柏洋郷|zh|柏洋乡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baiyang Township|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tahu Village (Xiapu County)|de|Tahu-Dorf (Xiapu County)|ja|塔後村 (霞浦県)|zh|塔后村|lt=Tahu Village|lt_ja=塔後村|lt_de=Tahu-Dorf|lt_zh=塔后村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahu Village (Xiapu County)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shangwan Village (Xiapu County)|de|Shangwan-Dorf (Xiapu County)|ja|上万村 (霞浦県)|zh|上万村|lt=Shangwan Natural Village|lt_ja=上万自然村|lt_de=Shangwan-Naturdorf|lt_zh=上万自然村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shangwan Village (Xiapu County)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It was established by Fujian {{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mingjiao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐代三夷教|1=Three Persian religions in Tang dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418516}} ([[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]]) follower {{ill|Sun Mian|de|Sun Mian|ja|孫綿|zh|孙绵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Mian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the {{ill|Northern Song|de|Nördliche Song-Dynastie|ja|北宋|zh|北宋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Song|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Northern Song dynasty|WD=Q319460}} period in the fourth year of {{ill|Qiande (Northern Song)|de|Qiande (Northern Song)|ja|乾德 (北宋)|zh|乾德|lt=Qiande|lt_ja=乾德|lt_de=Qiande|lt_zh=乾德|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qiande (Northern Song)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (966 AD), making it the earliest Manichaean building constructed in Fujian. For over a thousand years, it served as an important venue for Manichaean activities in the Baiyang area until it was destroyed in 2006 by {{ill|Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|de|Taifun Sangmei (2006)|ja|颱風桑美 (2006年)|zh|颱風桑美|lt=Sangmei Typhoon|lt_ja=桑美台風|lt_de=Sangmei-Taifun|lt_zh=桑美台风|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |author=樊丽沙 |author2=杨富学 |title=霞浦摩尼教文献及其重要性 |journal=世界宗教研究 |language=zh |publisher=中国社会科学院世界宗教研究所 |publication-place=北京 |publication-date=2011 |issue=06 |issn=1000-4289}}</ref>
<ref name="Kósa2018">{{Cite book|last=Kósa|first=Gábor|title=Der östliche Manichäismus im Spiegel seiner Buch- und Schriftkultur|publisher=Walter de Gruyter GmbH & Co KG|date=2018|isbn=978-3-11-059393-8|language=de}}</ref>

== Name ==
Leshan Hall was originally established as "Longshou Temple" and was renamed "Leshan Hall" during the Yuan dynasty. Initially, the name "Leshan" was believed to be derived from the phrase "The wise delight in water, and the benevolent delight in mountains" found in [[:en:Analects|The Analects]] (Lunyu: Yong Ye). Another theory suggests that the term "Leshan" originates from {{ill|Middle Persian|de|Mittelpersisch|ja|中古ペルシア語|zh|中古波斯语|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Persian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Lang|pal|Rōšan}} (or {{Lang|pal|Rōšn}}), meaning "light", which corresponds with the Manichaean doctrine that venerates light. Moreover, since the building was more likely used as a dining hall for followers rather than as a monastery (see the "Architectural Style and Nature" section), the change from "Temple" (寺) to "Hall" (堂) was probably due to this reason.
<ref name=":0" />

== Historical Development ==
According to the explicit records in the "Sun Family Genealogy of the Republic of China" compiled in the 21st year of the Republic (1932), Sun Mian from Chanyang Village (now also known as Shenyang Village, under the jurisdiction of Baiyang Township) purchased the land during the fourth year of {{ill|Qiande (Northern Song)|de|Qiande (Northern Song)|ja|乾德 (北宋)|zh|乾德|lt=Qiande|lt_ja=乾德|lt_de=Qiande|lt_zh=乾德|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qiande (Northern Song)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (966 AD) and commenced the construction of Longshou Temple, becoming its first abbot. In the fifth year of {{ill|Tiansheng|de|Tiansheng|ja|天聖|zh|天圣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiansheng|1=Tiansheng|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939230}} (1027 AD), a resident of Shangwan Village, {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, converted to Mingjiao at Longshou Temple, later becoming the leading missionary master of Mingjiao in the area and the second abbot of Longshou Temple. His direct disciple, Chen Pingshan, succeeded him.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>

During the Yuan dynasty, Longshou Temple was renamed Leshan Hall, and during the Ming and Qing dynasties, Leshan Hall underwent two major renovations. In the early years of the People's Republic of China, according to local elders, Leshan Hall still had several courtyards. At the beginning of the [[:en:Cultural Revolution|Cultural Revolution]], Leshan Hall was demolished and subsequently received only simple repairs with makeshift shed-like structures used for storing villagers' farming tools and for temporary shelter from rain. In 2006, Leshan Hall was destroyed by the {{ill|Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|de|Taifun Sangmei (2006)|ja|颱風桑美 (2006年)|zh|颱風桑美|lt=Super Typhoon Sangmei|lt_ja=超強台風桑美|lt_de=Super-Taifun Sangmei|lt_zh=超强台风桑美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leaving only the foundation columns, steps, and the base of the shrine.
<ref name=":0" />

== Architectural Style and Nature ==
Due to its great antiquity and the lack of written records, the original appearance of Leshan Hall is unknown. The site covers an area of approximately 1560 square meters. Based on photographs of the makeshift structure built at the end of the Cultural Revolution, Leshan Hall relied on columns for support and had no walls on any side. If this construction method was similar to the original, then Leshan Hall would not have been suitable for monastic practice; furthermore, its location at the village entrance, where followers tended to gather, rather than in a secluded area suitable for monks, indicates that Leshan Hall did not possess the typical characteristics of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Temple]], but rather those of a dining hall. This is one possible reason for the renaming from Longshou Temple to Leshan Hall.
<ref name=":0" />

== Related Activities ==
Every year on the {{ill|Ghost Festival|de|Geisterfest|ja|中元節|zh|中元节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ghost Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中元|1=Ulambana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q696781}} (the fifteenth day of the seventh month of the {{ill|Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender|ja|農暦|zh|农历|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国暦|1=Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134032}}), the Manichaean believers of Shangwan Village gather at Leshan Hall to perform a ritual for the transference of the souls of the deceased. This concept of soul transference is at odds with the Manichaean doctrine that venerates light, indicating that Manichaeism in Xiapu, in order to become popular and endure locally, had incorporated some unique elements—such as this soul-transference ritual—that provided comfort to the people.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>

  A collection of important documents on Manichaeism in Xiapu, it is currently the longest extant collection of Manichaean Chinese texts, with an account of Leshan Hall found in the manuscript "Leshan Hall Divine Record".
* {{ill|Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|de|Manichäische Steinskulpturen aus Shangwan Village|ja|上万村摩尼教石彫造像|zh|上万村摩尼教石雕造像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Built near Leshan Hall to adorn the Three Buddha Pagoda behind the tower and the Dengdong Stone Pagoda, it is one of the few surviving examples of Manichaean stone carving art.
* {{ill|Fushou Palace in Puxi|de|Fushou-Palast in Puxi|ja|浦西境福寿宮|zh|浦西境福寿宫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushou Palace in Puxi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Located outside the south gate of [[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou City]], it is the best-preserved Manichaean palace building, enshrining Lin Deng who once aided in disaster relief in Fuzhou.

== Historical Development ==
According to the explicit records in the "Sun Family Genealogy of the Republic of China" compiled in the 21st year of the Republic (1932), Sun Mian from Chanyang Village (now also known as Shenyang Village, under the jurisdiction of Baiyang Township) purchased the land during the fourth year of {{ill|Qiande (Northern Song)|de|Qiande (Northern Song)|ja|乾德 (北宋)|zh|乾德|lt=Qiande|lt_ja=乾德|lt_de=Qiande|lt_zh=乾德|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qiande (Northern Song)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (966 AD) and commenced the construction of Longshou Temple, becoming its first abbot. In the fifth year of {{ill|Tiansheng|de|Tiansheng|ja|天聖|zh|天圣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiansheng|1=Tiansheng|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939230}} (1027 AD), a resident of Shangwan Village, {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, converted to Mingjiao at Longshou Temple, later becoming the leading missionary master of Mingjiao in the area and the second abbot of Longshou Temple. His direct disciple, Chen Pingshan, succeeded him.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}}</ref>

During the Yuan dynasty, Longshou Temple was renamed Leshan Hall, and during the Ming and Qing dynasties, Leshan Hall underwent two major renovations. In the early years of the People's Republic of China, according to local elders, Leshan Hall still had several courtyards. At the beginning of the [[:en:Cultural Revolution|Cultural Revolution]], Leshan Hall was demolished and subsequently received only simple repairs with makeshift shed-like structures used for storing villagers' farming tools and for temporary shelter from rain. In 2006, Leshan Hall was destroyed by the {{ill|Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|de|Taifun Sangmei (2006)|ja|颱風桑美 (2006年)|zh|颱風桑美|lt=Super Typhoon Sangmei|lt_ja=超強台風桑美|lt_de=Super-Taifun Sangmei|lt_zh=超强台风桑美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Sangmei (2006)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leaving only the foundation columns, steps, and the base of the shrine.
<ref name=":0" />

== Architectural Style and Nature ==
Due to its great antiquity and the lack of written records, the original appearance of Leshan Hall is unknown. The site covers an area of approximately 1560 square meters. Based on photographs of the makeshift structure built at the end of the Cultural Revolution, Leshan Hall relied on columns for support and had no walls on any side. If this construction method was similar to the original, then Leshan Hall would not have been suitable for monastic practice; furthermore, its location at the village entrance, where followers tended to gather, rather than in a secluded area suitable for monks, indicates that Leshan Hall did not possess the typical characteristics of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Temple]], but rather those of a dining hall. This is one possible reason for the renaming from Longshou Temple to Leshan Hall.
<ref name=":0" />

== Related Activities ==
Every year on the {{ill|Ghost Festival|de|Geisterfest|ja|中元節|zh|中元节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ghost Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中元|1=Ulambana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q696781}} (the fifteenth day of the seventh month of the {{ill|Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender|ja|農暦|zh|农历|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国暦|1=Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134032}}), the Manichaean believers of Shangwan Village gather at Leshan Hall to perform a ritual for the transference of the souls of the deceased. This concept of soul transference is at odds with the Manichaean doctrine that venerates light, indicating that Manichaeism in Xiapu, in order to become popular and endure locally, had incorporated some unique elements—such as this soul-transference ritual—that provided comfort to the people.
<ref>{{Cite journal |author=马小鹤 |author2=吴春明 |title=摩尼教与济度亡灵——霞浦明教《奏申牒书科册》研究 |journal=九州学林 |language=zh |publication-date=2010 |issue=03 |page=45-46}}</ref>

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu-Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  A collection of important documents on Manichaeism in Xiapu, it is currently the longest extant collection of Manichaean Chinese texts, with an account of Leshan Hall found in the manuscript "Leshan Hall Divine Record".
* {{ill|Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|de|Manichäische Steinskulpturen aus Shangwan Village|ja|上万村摩尼教石彫造像|zh|上万村摩尼教石雕造像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manichaean Stone Carvings of Shangwan Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Built near Leshan Hall to adorn the Three Buddha Pagoda behind the tower and the Dengdong Stone Pagoda, it is one of the few surviving examples of Manichaean stone carving art.
* {{ill|Fushou Palace in Puxi|de|Fushou-Palast in Puxi|ja|浦西境福寿宮|zh|浦西境福寿宫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fushou Palace in Puxi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}  
  Located outside the south gate of [[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou City]], it is the best-preserved Manichaean palace building, enshrining Lin Deng who once aided in disaster relief in Fuzhou.
